title: encyclia insidiosa
intro: Encyclia insidiosa, also known as the venomous orchid, is a type of rare and toxic orchid found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant contains a toxin called calanemine, which has been shown to have medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various types of cancer, including leukemia and lymphoma. The health benefits of Encyclia insidiosa include its potential to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and stimulate the production of white blood cells. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by extracting the calanemine from the orchid's roots and leaves, which can then be used to create medicines and supplements.
